Happy Birthday Michael Caine

# Happy Birthday Michael Caine

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Mar 14, 2026

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

5

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

Sir Michael Caine, born Maurice Joseph Micklewhite on March 14, 1933, is a legendary British actor who retired in 2023 after a career spanning over 60 years and more than 130 films. At 93 years old, he remains celebrated for his versatility, iconic cockney accent, and two Academy Awards for Best Supporting Actor. He got his screen name from The Caine Mutiny, which had his favorite hero Humphrey Bogart in it.

These are some of my favorite movies of his as a hero, which I grew up watching, and enjoy even now.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!uF8n!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff2cb202c-fee1-4c90-bde5-daf8e163e23c_602x384.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!uF8n!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff2cb202c-fee1-4c90-bde5-daf8e163e23c_602x384.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The Man Who Would Be King, based on Rudyard Kipling’s novel, about two British army deserters, Peachy Carnehan( Michael Caine) and Daniel Dravot( Sean Connery), who find themselves in Kafiristan. The locals consider Dravot to be a God, and make him the King of their land. Unabashed escapist entertainment, Caine and Connery are just brilliant in this cheeky satire on colonialism, classic cinema at it’s best.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oB0r!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4e3ff06b-cf77-4b74-b86c-45d71f04791a_602x339.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oB0r!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4e3ff06b-cf77-4b74-b86c-45d71f04791a_602x339.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Zulu the movie that was Michael Caine’s first major role, based on Battle of Rorke’s Drift during the Anglo-Zulu War, Caine plays Lt Gonville Bromhead, an upper-class officer, who is part of a British mission station, that faces an attack from around 3000 Zulu warriors. Typical Hollywood period epic, with an epic sweep, great battle scenes and a rousing soundtrack by John Barry.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WDpa!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7e768f99-523b-4af8-905e-9dbde832baf5_602x408.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WDpa!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7e768f99-523b-4af8-905e-9dbde832baf5_602x408.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!RPLP!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F95fa098c-34c5-4fc8-962a-49b1a99daed8_602x401.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!RPLP!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F95fa098c-34c5-4fc8-962a-49b1a99daed8_602x401.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!iW-k!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa5e7120f-8d40-42d9-8819-acf6d450dee8_300x168.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!iW-k!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa5e7120f-8d40-42d9-8819-acf6d450dee8_300x168.jpeg)

The Harry Palmer trilogy of The Ipcress File, Funeral in Berlin and Billion Dollar Brain, the anti James Bond. Unlike the flashy guns, gadgets, glitz world of Bond, this trilogy is more down beat, realistic, gritty, all based on Len Deighton novels. Where the Ipcress File deals with Harry Palmer investigating the kidnapping of scientists, uncovering a brainwashing conspiracy, Funeral in Berlin is about the defection of a Soviet officer, filled with intrigue, double crossing and Billion Dollar Brain revolves around a plot involving a rogue anti Soviet organization, which compared to the first two was more glitzy.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!-Rax!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F549bfdbc-9e9a-4b1c-a8a4-0fd343d4c7e6_602x339.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!-Rax!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F549bfdbc-9e9a-4b1c-a8a4-0fd343d4c7e6_602x339.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The original Italian Job in 1969, still remains the best, compared to it’s remake. It’s tongue in cheek humor, not taking itself too seriously, and of course that epic car chase sequence in Turin, makes it a classic.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!nBh_!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2442fd1c-f626-4901-9dca-146ed2ad7736_602x361.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!nBh_!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2442fd1c-f626-4901-9dca-146ed2ad7736_602x361.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Get Carter is one of the best gangster movies ever, with Caine as Jack Carter, a ruthless London mob hitman, who begins to investigate his brother’s suspicious death in Newcastle, uncovering a sinister world of pornography rings, corruption leading to a rather violent, bleak ending. There was a forgettable remake of this in 2000 with Sly Stallone.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!LrHW!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7d2cdbe2-9442-4ea4-a322-8f9c62b596d0_602x339.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!LrHW!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7d2cdbe2-9442-4ea4-a322-8f9c62b596d0_602x339.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Sleuth a brilliant pyschological thriller, featuring just two actors Michael Caine and Sir Laurence Olivier, based on Anthony Shaffer’s Tony Award-winning play. Andrew Wyke played by Olivier,a wealthy mystery writer invites Milo Tindle played by Caine, his wife’s lover and proposes a scheme where Milo should stage a robbery of his wife’s jewellery. The plot leads to a series of mind games , a very highly cerebral thiller.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ArAp!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1b267cbc-7dc3-40d0-93d7-d5f49f846558_415x220.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ArAp!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1b267cbc-7dc3-40d0-93d7-d5f49f846558_415x220.jpeg)

The Eagle has Landed, based on Jack Higgins novel, featuring a rather outlandish plot by the Nazis to kidnap Winston Churchill. Caine shares screen space with Robert Duvall, Donald Pleasance as Nazi officers and Donald Sutherland as an IRA Agent, in this higly engaging WW2 drama, that has some great writing and superb performances.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!qqpF!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff2ea06bd-6679-45f5-b929-92e33ccd9dc4_602x432.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!qqpF!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff2ea06bd-6679-45f5-b929-92e33ccd9dc4_602x432.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Escape to Victory has Michael Caine leading a football team made up of Allied POWs against a German team, that includes an ensemble cast of Sly Stallone, Max Von Sydow and real life footballers Pele, Bobby Moore. This was pure escapist entertainment, absolute fun, ignore the rather silly plot and just soak in the excitement, as well as the football game. If you are a football fan, you gonna enjoy this.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4as2!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7bddac97-c39d-454c-9381-d7887bf67728_602x326.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4as2!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7bddac97-c39d-454c-9381-d7887bf67728_602x326.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Michael Caine and Steve Martin as two con men, competing with each other to swindle a wealthy tourist in the French Riveira. Dirty Rotten Scoundrels is one of the best comedies of the 80s, and both Caine, Martin are brilliant.

Not an exhaustive list but these are some of the best Caine movies I have seen and enjoyed. I have not seen Alfie one of his more acclaimed ones.

Of course his later performances in The Cider House Rules, Quiet American, Secondhand Lions, Children of Men, Harry Brown, Now You See Me and all those Nolan movies- The Prestige, Batman Begins, The Dark Knight, Inception, Interstellar would need a separate post.

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

5

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
